---
title: "Internet Use"
author: "Zaray"
date: "2023-09-02"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
library(broom)
library(knitr)
```

## R Markdown

# Load the necessary libraries
library(broom)
library(knitr)

# Create a data frame with the Internet Use data
data <- data.frame(
  "Internet Use" = c(
    "Individuals using the Internet (% of population)",
    "Individuals using the Internet (% of population)",
    "Individuals using the Internet (% of population)"
  ),
  "Country Name" = c("Mexico", "Canada", "United States"),
  "Country Code" = c("MEX", "CAN", "USA"),
  "2010" = c(31.05, 80.30, 71.69),
  "2011" = c(37.17630, 83.00000, 69.72946),
  "2012" = c(39.75, 83.00, 74.70),
  "2013" = c(43.46, 85.80, 71.40),
  "2014" = c(44.39, 87.12, 73.00),
  "2015" = c(57.43104, 90.00000, 74.55420),
  "2016" = c(59.54045, 91.16000, 85.54442),
  "2017" = c(53.03179, 92.70137, 87.27489),
  "2018" = c(56.65957, 94.64000, 88.49890),
  "2019" = c(69.63211, 91.91290, 89.43028),
  "2020" = c(71.49017, 92.30000, 90.62047),
  "2021" = c(75.62650, 92.83402, 91.75321)
)

# Perform bivariate regression for each country and tidy the results
tidy_results <- by(data, data$`Country Code`, function(df) {
  model <- lm(`X2021` ~ `X2010`, data = df)
  tidy(model)
})

# Combine the tidied results into a single data frame
tidy_results_df <- do.call(rbind, tidy_results)

# Rename the columns for clarity
colnames(tidy_results_df) <- c("Country", "Intercept", "Slope")

# Create a markdown table
kable(tidy_results_df, format = "markdown")
